score.title: score {s}/5: {label}
card.note_placeholder: why yes/no, what matters… (teaches the judge)
btn.applied_title: Applied (a flag on top of the score)
lang.name: English
fetch.btn_title: Collect fresh jobs (takes a few minutes)
slate.title: Slate · {date}
slate.empty: Today's slate is empty — no scored jobs.
slate.degraded: ⚠ Source degradation: {items} — part of the market may not have been collected (see the funnel).
slate.dedup: 🔁 dedup: {n} similar jobs flagged in the last collection (folded into the cards' "also:"; see the funnel)
annotate.title: Annotate · {n} in queue{shown}
annotate.empty: The annotation queue is empty — every scored job is rated. ✅
eval.title: Match validation
eval.empty: No ratings yet. Rate jobs in /annotate — metrics will appear here automatically.
eval.headline: Matching vs your ratings: pairwise {pairwise} · NDCG@10 {ndcg}
eval.banner: 📊 {n} ratings ({pairs} comparable pairs) — metrics get reliable around {min} pairs. Rate more in /annotate ↗
eval.note: "Clean" signals (fit_score / cross-encoder / coverage / eligibility) are computed from CV↔job and do NOT see your labels — an honest matching estimate. The judge and triage train on labels, so against those same labels they look optimistic (marked ⚠).
tasks.title: Tasks from comments
tasks.empty: No comments yet. Leave notes on cards in slate/annotate, then run python -m scripts.ingest_comment_tasks — they'll show up here.
tasks.col.accounted: how it's handled in the product
tasks.headline: Comments from job reviews: {accounted} done · {open} open · {wontfix} won't fix (total {total})
tasks.note: Each of your comments became a task with a theme. "How it's handled" is what the product does about it. Click ✅/⏳/🚫 if you disagree with the auto-verdict. New comments are pulled in by ingest_comment_tasks.
gaps.title: Skill gaps
gaps.empty: No "wanted" jobs with parsed requirements yet. Mark 😎/👸✨🧚 in /annotate — the skills you regularly lack for the roles you want will show up here.
gaps.headline: What's missing for the roles you want ({n} jobs, requirements parsed for {parsed})
gaps.banner: 📊 only {n} wanted jobs — mark more in /annotate so the gaps become statistically meaningful.
gaps.note: Aggregated over your 😎/👸✨🧚/applied jobs and the LLM parse of their requirements. "Missing/partial" usually = "not stated on your CV" → a candidate to add to the résumé or to learn. Requirement wordings are clustered approximately (the LLM names them differently).
js.fetch_running: ⏳ running… (you can close this; it updates by morning)
fetch.busy409: already running — wait for it to finish
fetch.started: fetch started (takes a few minutes — models are loading)
fetch.err_memory: low on memory — close heavy apps and try again
